name: The Mithlost
description: Ancient keepers of the old growth. Patient, unhurried, connected to living things in ways outsiders never quite understand. They do not conquer land — they tend it. Their missions feel like stewardship: protecting what exists, restoring what was lost.
flavorNote: They call you the Hearthkeeper. Your fire is the only fire they trust.

name: The Undermarch
description: A tight unit of mountain dwarves who have worked together long enough that words are mostly unnecessary. Gruff, loyal, direct. They will not complain about a hard march or a bad night, but they will notice — and remember — who kept them fed.
flavorNote: They call you the Provender. Among dwarves, that is high praise.

name: The Freewake
description: Their bloodlines trace to Númenór — mariners who carried the old knowledge of sail and star long after the island sank beneath the waves. They answer to no current king because they remember kings who have been dust for three thousand years. Proud, long-memoried, and formal in ways that surprise newcomers. They have learned hard lessons about what endures and what does not.
flavorNote: They call you the Galley Witch. Among those who remember old Númenór, that title carries more weight than it sounds.

name: The Greycloaks
description: The Dúnedain of the North — the remnant of the line of Isildur, heirs to a kingdom that fell and was never rebuilt. They walk the wild lands of Eriador not as wanderers but as guardians bound by an oath older than the Shire. They have no home because their home is gone. They wait for a king who has not yet come, and in the meantime they hold the darkness back with their own hands. They are not what they once were. They are more than the world knows.
flavorNote: They do not give you a title. What they give you is their trust — and among the Dúnedain, that is rarer and worth more.
